The '''RH7 CardShark''' was a [[Dealer droid/Legends|sabacc-dealing droid]] made by [[LeisureMech Enterprises/Legends|LeisureMech Enterprises]]. They were installed in [[Casino/Legends|casinos]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a replacement for organic dealers.

==Description==
The RH7 CardShark was a [[Dealer droid/Legends|dealer droid]] specialized for the card game [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] hung down from the ceiling, with a wide, rounded base bolted directly above the gaming table. The main section of the droid consisted of the dealer mechanism, which could raise and lower itself to a maximum extension of one and half [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. The CardShark was equipped with specialized [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor arrays]], an orifice resembling a mouth that was used to store sabacc cards, and two [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]] to deal the cards with. The droid also had a [[Card-flux Pulse Emitter]] installed to randomize the values on the sabacc cards.<ref name="EGTD" />

Six surveillance eyes ringed the rounded base that were used to monitor the sabacc players' hands, watching for any evidence of cheating. If the droid discovered that a player was using a [[skifter]] card, two heavy [[Grasper arm/Legends|grasper arms]] descended to remove the player. Modified [[Force pike/Legends|force pikes]] capable of delivering moderate [[Stun/Legends|stun]] shocks could be deployed in order to keep other players away from the cheater, or to protect the dealer droid until [[Bouncer (occupation)/Legends|bouncers]] could respond to the incident.<ref name="EGTD" />

==Role==
RH7 CardSharks were intended to replace organic dealers in gambling establishments.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|98|RH7 CardShark}}</ref> The dealer droid controlled the sabacc game, announcing the house rules and the variant being played, such as [[Bespin Standard]] or [[Empress Teta Preferred/Legends|Empress Teta Preferred]], before play began. During the game, the CardShark shuffled and dealt the cards, and initiated rounds of betting. During play, the pulse emitter randomly sent out electronic pulses to the cards to shift their values unless they had been placed in the gaming table's [[interference field]].<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
RH7 CardSharks were manufactured by [[LeisureMech Enterprises/Legends|LeisureMech Enterprises]]. The [[Crystal Jewel]] [[Casino/Legends|casino]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] employed RH7 CardSharks. In some situations, the droid was directed to cheat the players at the direction of the casino to ensure the house won. This was a risky proposition for the casino; if players discovered or suspected that the droid was commonly cheating them, it had no defense against a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt.<ref name="EGTD" />
